title: Causal stint-curvature features instead of static wear levels
summary: Recent tyre-degradation work models wear as a latent, time-varying process and explicitly allows degradation rate to accelerate.
rationale: Recent tyre-degradation work models wear as a latent, time-varying process and explicitly allows degradation rate to accelerate. That points to recent slope and curvature within a stint being more informative than absolute tyre age alone.
implementation_hint: Using trailing windows only within `Race_Year` + `Driver` + `Stint`, compute robust slopes/accelerations for `LapTime_Delta`, `LapTime (s)`, and `Cumulative_Degradation`; add deviation from the first 2-3 laps of the stint and a ‘degradation cliff’ flag when recent slope steepens materially.
expected_effect: Should move positives earlier in rank order when a driver is entering a real pit window but has not yet crossed a simple tyre-life threshold.
risk: Out-laps, traffic, or caution-like laps can mimic a cliff; windowing must be strictly past-only after correct race ordering.
